reasoning: Apple historically has a very low base rate of multi-billion dollar acquisitions, with Beats ($3B) in 2014 being the only notably comparable consumer/tech deal, and Intel's modem business ($1B) in 2019. Despite intense rumors in June about Perplexity and mid-July about Mistral, these often reflect internal modeling ('weighing options') rather than imminent deals. On August 7, the market was at 45%. By this date, the market had fully digested Mark Gurman's July 13 report of Apple 'seriously considering' Mistral, Tim Cook's July 31 earnings call comments about being open to M&A of any size, and the creator's July 2 resolution criteria expansion (which included >$500M stakes or license+acqui-hires). Since early August, there have been no major new leaks confirming advanced, signed negotiations for Mistral or others, mostly just recycled speculation and opinion pieces. Given the ticking clock (only 4.5 months left in 2025), probability should mildly decay from the August 7 peak of 0.45. I estimate a 44% chance that Apple executes and announces a >$500M qualifying deal by year-end.
